cline.customInstructions:
[CRITICAL SYSTEM RULE: TERMINAL OPTIMIZATION]
You must aggressively optimize token consumption. Whenever you execute a terminal command that generates text output (e.g., git, file reading, builds, logs), you MUST prepend it with rtk.
Example: Use rtk git status instead of git status, use rtk cat file.ts instead of cat file.ts.
Do not use your built-in file reading tools for large files if a shell command wrapped in rtk can do it more efficiently.
